title: Document Checklist for client - is it possible to integrate the list in Monday and send updates to client?
excerpt: We require a list of documents from prospective clients to prepare their offer.  A secure delivery method is needed along with the ability to check the received documents off the list with a received date.  It would be g…

posts.author: bernie0932 (Bernie Dandridge)
posts.cookedHtml: We require a list of documents from prospective clients to prepare their offer. A secure delivery method is needed along with the ability to check the received documents off the list with a received date. It would be great if the system could email or text the client with an updated list. Can Monday or one or more of their application providers help with this redundant task? (We do have to review the document to check to make sure it satisfies the requirement). Thanks for your response.

posts.author: lamb12 (Laura ELIZABETH Lamb)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Bernie - did you get an answer to this please? I am looking to do the same and struggling! Many thanks

posts.author: bernie0932 (Bernie Dandridge)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello Laura Elizabeth, Unfortunately Monday did not respond with any assistance. I have looked around for a solution and currently I am investigating FileInvite. They seem to have a good following in my industry. you can find them at www. fileinvite.com. All the best to you.

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml:
Bernie Dandridge:

We require a list of documents from prospective clients to prepare their offer. A secure delivery method is needed along with the ability to check the received documents off the list with a received date. It would be great if the system could email or text the client with an updated list. Can Monday or one or more of their application providers help with this redundant task? (We do have to review the document to check to make sure it satisfies the requirement). Thanks for your response.

Hello @bernie0932

I believe, this can be done pretty nicely.

Here’s what I would suggest

  • Create a task let’s say “Documents required from the client” and add all the names of the documents as columns (that you can check/un-check)
  • Design an automation to email the client when this task is populated initially to send an automated email. You can formulate the text in a way to fetch the individual columns of the document to depict their status in the email
  • Also design a form where the client can submit their document (all of them together or one by one by multiple submissions, whatever logic is more suited)
  • Assign a new column to capture the date by when all of these documents should be in
  • Design another automation that checks for this newly due date column, If it is due today, and all document column against this task are not marked checked, drop another email as a reminder with the updated status of the documents.

Now is this workflow perfect? it might not be, as I dont have the complete picture of your workflow and business, but this should work temporarily.

If you’re able to do this, great. Otherwise, you can engage a Monday consultant to help you out design your workflow along with this (and other) automations and I am sure you can create a great system.

Happy to help.

Zaki
Official Monday.com partner and Ambassador
Ready Logic

posts.author: WirkKarl (Karl)
posts.cookedHtml: Great question, Bernie. While Monday does offer some automation and CRM integrations, you might also want to look into Phonexa — it’s a robust platform that supports marketing automation, lead tracking, and client communication workflows. You could build out secure client intake processes, automate reminders, and sync document checklists in a streamlined way. It might complement your current setup really well.
